text:
So I built the room.

Not a question bank. Not a flashcard app. Not another way to memorize pathways.

A room. With a patient in it. A real case, a real history, real numbers on a real monitor. And a clock that moves whether you are ready or not. Because in medicine the clock always moves.

You walk in. You talk to the patient. You order the labs. You read what comes back. You treat what you find. And an AI attending teaches you while you work, using the actual numbers in front of you.

Not a textbook case. This case. This patient. This potassium of 6.2 at this moment in this presentation.

And if you get it wrong, something happens. The pressure keeps falling. The rhythm changes. The physiology responds the way real physiology does.

Because consequences are the teacher. And this room can hold them.
